senses.definition: feeling jealous of another's advantages, possessions, or experiences that one lacks

examples.x: He was green with envy when he found out that his classmate had received a scholarship to a prestigious university.
His face has been green with envy ever since his neighbor bought a brand new sports car.
I can already imagine her turning green with envy when she sees her sister's beautiful wedding dress.
When she saw her friend's luxurious vacation photos, she turned green with envy, wishing she could experience such luxury.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who keeps repeating the same thing in a way that is annoying

examples.x: He'd walk around the house repeating his favourite sayings like a broken record.

senses.definition: someone who does or says things that prevent others from having a good time

examples.x: Don't invite him to the party; he's such a wet blanket and will ruin the fun for everyone.
If he continues to be a wet blanket, he might not get invited to future social events.
She always plays the role of a wet blanket in team meetings, pointing out potential problems without offering solutions.
Their constant complaints and negativity make them act like a wet blanket, bringing down the whole group.

senses.definition: a person who is unhappy and keeps complaining a lot

examples.x: His constant pessimism and complaints make him come across as a misery guts to everyone.
No matter the situation, she always acts like a misery guts, never finding anything to be happy about.
Why are you being such a misery guts today?

senses.definition: a person who can no longer feel any strong emotions, often after experiencing something heartbreaking

examples.x: Her obsession with material possessions has turned her into an empty shell, ignoring the true values in life.
If he doesn't address his feelings, he may become an empty shell, unable to connect with others.
sed to be full of life, but lately, he seems like an empty shell, just going through the motions.

senses.definition: one's lack of courage to deal with painful or dangerous situations

examples.x: Don't count on him to stand up for you; he has a yellow streak and avoids confrontation.
His yellow streak prevents him from taking risks or trying new experiences.
If he overcomes his fears, he can conquer his yellow streak and become more confident.
When faced with a dangerous task, he showed his yellow streak and refused to participate.

senses.definition: easily frightened or discouraged

examples.x: Her constant worries and hesitations show that she is faint of heart, unable to take risks.
In the past, he faced the dangerous journey with determination, proving he was not faint of heart.
The haunted house attraction is not suitable for the faint of heart; it's designed to scare people.
You need to be brave to try skydiving; it's not for the faint of heart.

word_list.pronunciation: ɐfɹˈeɪd skˈɛɹd fɹˈaɪʔn̩d ʌv wˈʌnz ˈoʊn ʃˈædoʊ

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who becomes extremely afraid of or nervous about even the smallest things

examples.x: He's afraid of his own shadow when it comes to making decisions; he's always seeking reassurance from others.
Her constant worry and fear make her act like she's frightened of her own shadow, which is holding her back from taking risks.
In the past, she was scared of her own shadow, but over time, she gained confidence and became more assertive.
The new employee is so afraid of her own shadow that she's hesitant to voice her opinions in meetings.

senses.definition: a person who doubts or refuses to believe anything that is presented to them without evidence or proof
used to refer to someone or something that is extremely quiet and does not make much noise

examples.x: The baby was finally asleep, and the house was quiet as a mouse.
I tried to be as quiet as a mouse
The cat tiptoed into the room, as quiet as a mouse, not waking anyone up.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who refuses to talk much or is completely silent

examples.x: Despite multiple attempts to engage him in conversation, he remains as mute as a fish, unwilling to communicate.
During the meeting, he remained mute as a fish, hardly saying a word.
If she overcomes her shyness, she won't be mute as a fish during social gatherings.
Whenever the topic of public speaking comes up, she becomes as mute as a fish and avoids speaking in front of others.

senses.definition: a person who is extremely worried or upset

examples.x: Her constant worrying and anxious behavior make her appear like a nervous wreck, seeking reassurance from others.
If she learns some stress-management techniques, she can avoid becoming a nervous wreck during challenging times.
She's a nervous wreck before exams, always worrying about her performance.
The high-pressure situation at work has turned him into a nervous wreck, affecting his performance.

senses.definition: a person who is always nervous or stressed and is therefore unable to have a calm and organized life

examples.x: After the tragic accident, he became a basket case, unable to recover emotionally.
If he seeks therapy and support, he may avoid becoming a basket case during difficult times.
She's been so overwhelmed with work and family issues that she feels like a complete basket case.

senses.definition: an extremely anxious or nervous person

examples.x: Before her performance, she was a bundle of nerves, worried about making any mistakes.
He's always a bundle of nerves before flying; he's afraid of turbulence.
Her constant worrying and fidgeting make her seem like a bag of nerves, unable to stay calm.
With relaxation techniques, he can manage his anxiety and avoid being a bag of nerves in stressful situations.

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt sˈeɪ bˈuː tʊ ɐ ɡˈuːs

senses.definition: to be very quiet and show signs of discomfort or anxiety when in public

examples.x: He's the quietest person I know, he wouldn't say boo to a goose.
His extreme shyness makes him appear as if he wouldn't say boo to a goose.
She's so shy that she wouldn't say boo to a goose, even in a small gathering.

word_list.word: like a bump on a log
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ɐ bˈʌmp ˌɑːn ɐ lˈɔɡ

senses.definition: used to refer to someone or something that shows very little motion or does nothing

examples.x: During the team brainstorming session, he sat there like a bump on a log, not offering any ideas or input.
Her lack of engagement during the event made her appear like a bump on a log.
If he actively participates, he won't be sitting like a bump on a log in the next team activity.
You need to be more involved in the project; don't just sit there like a bump on a log.

senses.definition: someone who sits around and watches TV a lot

examples.x: Don't be such a couch potato; get up and go for a walk!
His unhealthy lifestyle of being a couch potato is affecting his overall well-being.
If he starts going for regular walks, he can avoid becoming a couch potato.
Instead of going outside and exercising, he chooses to be a couch potato all day.
His lack of physical activity and constant TV watching have turned him into a couch potato.

senses.definition: a negative attitude or reaction toward something that one desires but cannot have or achieve, often by minimizing its importance or worth

examples.x: After not getting the job, he dismissed it as a boring position with a low salary, exhibiting sour grapes.
His constant criticism of others' achievements is just sour grapes due to his lack of success.
If she doesn't get the promotion, it's important not to exhibit sour grapes and continue to work hard.
She said the concert was probably going to be terrible anyway after failing to get tickets, displaying sour grapes.

senses.definition: the feeling of discontent caused by lacking someone else's possessions, accomplishments, etc.

examples.x: Her possessiveness and suspiciousness are clear signs of the green-eyed monster in her.
His constant jealousy towards his partner is like a green-eyed monster, causing problems in their relationship.
In the past, his green-eyed monster led to conflicts and misunderstandings with his colleagues.
She couldn't hide her green-eyed monster when she saw her best friend spending time with someone else.

senses.definition: to have a tendency to steal from others whenever the opportunity presents itself

examples.x: Don't leave your belongings unattended around him; he's known to have sticky fingers.
Her habit of taking things that don't belong to her shows that she has sticky fingers.
If he learns the value of honesty, he won't have sticky fingers in the future.
The security team suspects that someone in the office has sticky fingers, as items keep going missing.

senses.definition: used to describe a person with no principles or morality

examples.x: His constant manipulation of others and lack of remorse make him seem lower than a snake's belly.
His deliberate betrayal of his friends is evidence that he's lower than a snake's belly.
I can't believe she lied to her own family; she's lower than a snake's belly.

senses.definition: a person who shows unwillingness to spend much money

examples.x: He's known in the office as a penny pincher, always finding ways to cut costs.

senses.definition: lacking good manners

examples.x: I tried to talk to her about it, but she's just hairy at the heels.
My boss is hairy at the heel.

senses.definition: to complain about something that is not worth complaining about

examples.x: Stop crying stinking fish about the price of milk!
I can't believe you're crying stinking fish about your new car.
Can't you just be happy with what you have instead of crying stinking fish?
